pages.raw_text:
Mr. Allen, representative of the
union linemen, said that the union
men, under ordinary circumstances,
would have refused to work on the
same building with the non-union
men employed by the Washington
Water Power company, but had de-
cided to go ahead, feeling that the
entire community would be hurt if
they refused to aid preparations for
the fair opening. They are doing a
class of work that the non-union
men cannot do, and say if the man.
ager of the fair will protect them
from abuse and insult at the hands
of the deputy police and sheriff !
they will finish the job, as the
lights are an absolute necessity to
the success Of the fair.


The action taken by Manager
Cosgrove in excluding from the
grounds the specials of the local
monopoly shows that he puts but
little reliance in the statements of
the Spokesman-Review and the
manager of the Washington Water
Power company that the union men
were responsible for the recent
trouble with the construction work
done by the non-union men at the
grounds. TO cover up the quality
of the work done, 1t was claimed
the wiring had been tempered with
by union men.
